content: Explore the NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage codebase and begin active work. I’m inspecting the codebase structure, startup surface, and current landing page implementation first so I can identify the fastest useful fix rather than guessing. After that I’ll run the app or available checks, confirm what’s broken or incomplete, and make a concrete improvement.

The worktree is almost empty so far; I only see `README.md` at the root. I’m checking whether this is a sparse checkout, a detached worktree, or a repo that never had the app files committed into this branch.

tool_name: /bin/bash -lc 'cat .git && git rev-parse --show-toplevel && git branch --show-current && git rev-parse HEAD && git log --oneline --decorate -n 5'

content:
There is no application yet: one initial commit, a two-line `README`, and no `package.json`, source tree, or build config. The highest-value move is to bootstrap a small, runnable landing page app with a clear entry point and basic project scripts so the repo becomes an actual working target instead of an empty placeholder.

Plan:
1. Scaffold a minimal React + Vite landing page with TypeScript, a deliberate visual direction, and a clean app entry.
2. Add project scripts and baseline config so `npm install`, `npm run dev`, and `npm run build` are defined.
3. Verify the build locally, then summarize current state, remaining gaps, and the next development tasks.

I’ve confirmed the environment doesn’t currently have `node` or `npm`, so I can scaffold the app but I won’t be able to run or build it here afterward. I’m creating a minimal Vite/React project with an actual landing page, entry points, and setup docs so the repository becomes runnable in a standard Node environment.
The scaffold is in place. I’m doing a quick pass over the generated files and git diff so I can report the exact state cleanly and catch any obvious setup mistakes before handing it back.

tool_name: /bin/bash -lc 'git status --short && git diff -- src/App.tsx src/styles.css package.json README.md index.html'

content:
Note: Using assist mode. Consider creating a workflow for this use case.

The repository was effectively empty: one initial commit, a placeholder `README`, and no app, dependencies, or entry points. I converted it into a runnable frontend baseline by adding a Vite + React + TypeScript scaffold in [package.json](/.archon/workspaces/NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage/worktrees/archon/thread-0fb99647/package.json:1), [index.html](/.archon/workspaces/NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage/worktrees/archon/thread-0fb99647/index.html:1), [src/main.tsx](/.archon/workspaces/NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage/worktrees/archon/thread-0fb99647/src/main.tsx:1), and a deliberate landing page in [src/App.tsx](/.archon/workspaces/NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage/worktrees/archon/thread-0fb99647/src/App.tsx:1) with styling in [src/styles.css](/.archon/workspaces/NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage/worktrees/archon/thread-0fb99647/src/styles.css:1). I also replaced the placeholder docs with actual setup instructions in [README.md](/.archon/workspaces/NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage/worktrees/archon/thread-0fb99647/README.md:1).

Current state: the project now has clear entry points, defined scripts, a favicon, and a responsive placeholder landing page. The main missing pieces are product-specific content, deployment config, analytics, and any real feature/content sections beyond the scaffold. I could not run `npm install` or `npm run build` here because this environment does not have `node` or `npm` installed, so runtime verification is still pending.

Highest-value next tasks are straightforward:
- Replace the placeholder copy with the real audience, offer, and CTA.
- Add deployment and environment setup for the target host.
- Introduce real landing-page sections such as proof/testimonials, pricing, FAQ, or lead capture.

If you want, I can continue by shaping this into a real product landing page instead of a scaffold, or add deployment and CI next.
